Built-in versus freestanding is the final fork in the road. A drop-in grill head like the Hygrill STD Series or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head integrates cleanly into a masonry or stainless island and connects directly to a natural gas line — no tank refills, no running out mid-cook. Freestanding island stations such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island bundle storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table into a single purchase, which lowers the total project cost if you don't already have an island structure. The trade-off is flexibility: built-in heads are permanent fixtures, while a complete station can technically be repositioned, though at 44,000 BTU and full cabinet weight that's rarely practical.

For buyers building a full outdoor cooking setup, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen goes deeper on integration considerations like cutout dimensions and ventilation requirements. If you're still deciding between natural gas and other fuel types or want a broader comparison of rated models, the natural gas outdoor grills hub covers the full category. Those planning a dedicated barbecue station should also check the best barbeque grills for natural gas for models spec'd specifically around low-and-slow cooking.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality natural gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a reputable freestanding natural gas grill. The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner model comes in at $519 and delivers a cabinet-style enclosure, infrared side sear burners, and a clearview lid at that entry point. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens start higher — the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head is $889.99, while premium options like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In run $1,129.99. Island station packages with storage and prep tables range from $799.99 to $1,399.99 depending on burner count and features.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner — is designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen island or cabinet cutout. It has no legs and no side shelves of its own. A freestanding grill, like the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner, comes mounted on a rolling cart or cabinet and needs no surrounding structure. Built-ins give a cleaner integrated look but require a permanent installation; freestanding units are quicker to set up and easier to reposition.

Can I convert a propane grill to natural gas?

Some models are designed specifically for dual-fuel flexibility. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Grill Head is explicitly listed as natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is labeled LPG/NG Convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill is sold to run on either propane or natural gas. Conversion typically requires a separate kit with different orifices sized for natural gas pressure — always confirm the kit is available before purchasing a propane-only unit with conversion in mind.

How much BTU output do I actually need?

For a 3- or 4-burner grill, 40,000–48,000 BTUs across the main burners is a practical target for high-heat searing and general cooking. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head delivers exactly 40,000 BTUs, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island Station steps up to 44,000 BTUs. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner tops the lineup at 48,000 BTUs on the main grate plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner. Raw BTU numbers matter less than burner-to-grate ratio — a smaller grill running 40,000 BTUs can outperform a larger one with the same output spread thin.

Is a flat-top griddle a good alternative to a traditional grate grill for natural gas outdoor kitchens?

It depends on what you cook most. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle offers a 34,000 BTU flat cooking surface that excels at breakfast foods, smash burgers, stir-fry, and anything that benefits from full surface contact. It loses the char and open-flame flavor of a grate grill. Many outdoor kitchens install both — a grill head on one side and a griddle insert on the other — which is practical given that the Stanbroil drops into a standard island cutout just like a built-in grill head.

What should I look for in grates when comparing natural gas grills?

Cast iron grates retain heat better and produce stronger sear marks but require seasoning and are prone to rust if neglected. Porcelain-coated grates — like those on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner — resist corrosion and are easier to clean, though the coating can chip over time if scraped aggressively. Stainless steel rod grates are the most durable and need the least maintenance, making them the standard on commercial-grade 304 stainless builds like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner. For a natural gas setup that stays outdoors year-round, stainless or porcelain-coated grates generally outlast bare cast iron in humid climates.

Three burners also means less granular zone control than four- or five-burner alternatives, which matters on longer grilling sessions where different proteins need different heat levels simultaneously. Spec details beyond burner count and fuel type are limited in available data, so buyers should request full BTU and cooking surface dimensions before committing.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output and burner count set the floor for what a flat top gas grill can actually do. A 2-burner griddle like the Stanbroil 28-Inch delivers 34,000 BTUs across a compact built-in footprint — enough for consistent searing across the full surface. Step up to a 4- or 5-burner island unit and the numbers climb fast: the Tytus Fresno pushes 48,000 BTUs on the main grate alone, plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner. More burners also mean independent heat zones, which matters when you're cooking proteins and vegetables simultaneously at different temperatures. For a flat top specifically, even heat distribution across the griddle plate is more important than raw BTU numbers — look for burners that run the full width of the cooking surface rather than concentrated spots underneath.

Material quality is the single biggest factor in longevity for an outdoor installation. Heavy-gauge 304 stainless steel resists corrosion far better than 430-grade alternatives, and it's worth confirming which grade a manufacturer is actually using. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head specifies Heavy Duty 304 stainless steel explicitly. For built-in or drop-in configurations — the natural fit for flat top grills — a welded stainless frame will outlast powder-coated steel by years in coastal or high-humidity environments. Lid design matters too: a tight-fitting lid retains heat for faster warm-up and doubles as weather protection when the grill is idle.

OUR TOP PICK

The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs nails the built-in drop-in form factor purpose-built for BBQ islands, a 34,000 BTU output tuned specifically for full-surface flat top cooking, and durable stainless steel construction with a lid included — all in a natural gas–ready package. $1109.99

VIEW ON AMAZON →

Fuel flexibility and installation type narrow the field quickly. Pure natural gas built-ins eliminate tank swaps and suit permanent outdoor kitchens; propane/NG convertible models like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island give flexibility if your setup might move or if natural gas isn't yet plumbed. Storage cabinets, fold-out prep tables, and side burners add meaningful utility to a flat top station — the Brand-Man Island includes storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table at $799.99, making it one of the more complete packages at that price. Factor in cooking area in square inches alongside BTUs: the Tytus Fresno's 864 sq. in. of cooking space changes the math on how many people you can realistically feed at once.

For broader context on building around a flat top grill, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchens guide covers built-in compatibility and island integration in depth. If you're still deciding between fuel types and want to compare the full natural gas lineup across all grill styles, the natural gas outdoor grills hub is the right starting point, and the best outdoor grills for natural gas roundup breaks down top-rated options across categories.

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between a flat top grill and a traditional grate grill?

A flat top grill uses a solid steel or cast iron cooking surface instead of open grates. That solid surface retains heat evenly across the entire cooking area, making it better suited for eggs, smash burgers, pancakes, stir-fry, and anything else that would fall through or flare up on a grate. The trade-off is that you lose the direct flame char marks that a grate grill produces. For outdoor kitchens where versatility across multiple food types matters, a flat top surface handles a broader cooking range. For pure steakhouse char, a grate grill or an infrared sear burner is the better tool.

What BTU output do I actually need for a flat top outdoor gas grill?

BTU output matters more for how quickly a griddle surface reaches cooking temperature than for sustained cooking performance. A 2-burner built-in unit like the Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle at 34,000 BTUs is adequate for a household cooking surface of that size. Larger island setups — like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner at 48,000 BTU main plus a 12,000 BTU side burner — are designed for high-volume outdoor cooking where multiple zones run simultaneously. As a practical floor, aim for at least 15,000 BTUs per burner on a flat top surface to ensure fast preheat and reliable high-heat searing.

How much should I expect to spend on a quality flat top outdoor gas grill?

Entry-level island-style flat top setups with storage start around $800, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island at $799.99. Mid-range built-in heads from brands like Hygrill and Stanbroil run $1,100–$1,130 for stainless steel construction with natural gas compatibility. Full island systems with cabinets, side burners, and semi-portable frames — like the Tytus Fresno at $1,399.99 — represent the upper tier before custom fabrication costs enter the picture. Budget at least $1,000 if 304 stainless steel construction and a built-in configuration are priorities.

Can I convert a propane flat top grill to natural gas?

Many flat top grills in this category are sold as dual-fuel or convertible units. The Brand-Man Built-In Head 30-Inch 4-Burner, for example, is listed as natural gas convertible despite being sold primarily as a propane unit. Conversion typically requires a separate orifice kit and, depending on the manufacturer, may affect the warranty if not done by a licensed technician. Always confirm that the specific model ships with or sells a compatible conversion kit before purchasing. If permanent natural gas connection is the plan from day one, buying a unit pre-configured for natural gas avoids the conversion step entirely. For more options designed around natural gas supply, see the Natural Gas Outdoor Grills guide.

What size flat top grill do I need for a built-in outdoor kitchen?

Built-in grill heads are sized by their drop-in cutout dimensions, not the overall unit width. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle requires a corresponding 28-inch cutout in the island counter, while the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head needs a 30-inch opening. Measure your island's available run carefully before ordering, and account for the surrounding counter material's heat tolerance. If the outdoor kitchen is still in the planning phase, sizing the island around the grill head — rather than the reverse — gives more flexibility. For full outdoor kitchen grill configurations, the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen page covers additional built-in options.

Is stainless steel construction important for a flat top outdoor grill?

Yes, particularly for built-in and permanent outdoor installations. A flat top surface is exposed to grease, moisture, and temperature cycling every time it is used, and lower-grade steel will pit and rust faster under those conditions. Units like the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In Grill and the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head both specify stainless steel construction, which resists corrosion in outdoor environments. 304-grade stainless — called out explicitly by Brand-Man on its built-in head — is the standard benchmark for outdoor kitchen durability. Regardless of material grade, a fitted cover and regular surface seasoning extend the lifespan of any flat top cooking surface significantly.

Buying guide and further reading

More burners spread that heat across a larger grate, so a 4-burner setup at 40,000 BTUs like the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head can still run distinct heat zones for searing and indirect cooking simultaneously. Match burner count and BTU distribution to how you actually cook, not to whichever number looks biggest on a spec sheet.

Construction grade separates a grill that survives five seasons from one that rusts out in two. Food-contact surfaces should be 304 stainless steel — the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head and Hygrill STD Series both use it. Cooking grates matter too: porcelain-coated cast iron, as found on the Tytus Fresno, retains heat well and releases food cleanly, but demands careful maintenance to avoid chipping. For a flat-top format, a cold-rolled steel griddle surface (the Stanbroil 28-Inch) develops a seasoned layer over time that rivals cast iron for non-stick performance. Either way, inspect welds, lid thickness, and drip-tray depth before committing.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality natural gas BBQ grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a full-featured freestanding natural gas grill with multiple burners and a stainless steel build. The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner model comes in at $519 and delivers infrared side sear burners, a Clearview lid, and a cabinet-style base at that entry price. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens start closer to $890, as seen with the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head at $889.99.

What's the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head is a drop-in unit designed to be framed into a custom outdoor kitchen counter — it has no legs or enclosure of its own. The Hygrill STD Series and Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head are both examples. A grill island is a self-contained station with its own cabinet base, storage, and sometimes a prep table. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill are full stations you can place on a patio without any construction.

Can propane grills be converted to natural gas?

Many models sold as propane-primary are convertible with the right orifice kit. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head is explicitly listed as natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is sold as LPG/NG convertible out of the box. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is also offered in a propane-or-natural-gas configuration. Always verify the conversion kit is included or available before purchasing — not every grill that runs on propane can safely be converted.

How much BTU output do I actually need for outdoor grilling?

For a four-burner grill cooking for six to eight people, 40,000–44,000 BTUs across the main burners is a practical target. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head delivers exactly 40,000 BTUs, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island reaches 44,000 BTUs. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner tops the group at 48,000 BTUs on the main grates plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner — useful when you want to run a sauce pot simultaneously. Raw BTU numbers matter less than burner coverage relative to grate area, so look at both figures together.

Is a flat-top griddle better than a traditional grate for a natural gas outdoor kitchen?

It depends on what you cook most. A flat-top griddle excels at smash burgers, breakfast foods, seafood, and anything that benefits from even surface contact and easy fat management. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle gives you a 34,000 BTU two-burner flat top with a lid designed specifically for outdoor kitchen drop-in installation. Traditional grated grills produce better char and smoke contact on steaks and larger cuts. Many serious outdoor kitchens install both side by side.

What size grill is right for a family of four versus entertaining large groups?

A family of four can work comfortably with a three-burner grill and roughly 450–500 sq. in. of primary cooking area. The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner covers that need as a built-in option. For regular entertaining of eight or more people, move to a five-burner format with expanded cooking surface — the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner offers 864 sq. in. of cooking space, which is enough to run proteins and vegetables simultaneously without rotating food off the grill. For outdoor kitchen builds intended for large gatherings, also consider pairing a grill head with a dedicated side burner or griddle station; see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) for configuration guidance.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality outdoor gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a full-featured freestanding grill. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 sits at that entry point and still delivers infrared side sear burners, a clearview lid, and a 5/5 rating across 71 reviews. Island-style grills with storage cabinets and side burners start closer to $800, as seen with the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Island at $799.99. Built-in grill heads for permanent outdoor kitchens typically run $889 and up.

What is the difference between a freestanding grill and a built-in grill head?

A freestanding grill is a complete unit on legs or a cart — move it, use it, done. A built-in grill head drops into a countertop or BBQ island surround and has no cabinet of its own; installation is permanent. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In Grill are designed specifically for drop-in installations. If you are still planning your outdoor kitchen layout, a freestanding island unit like the Tytus Fresno or Brand-Man Island Grill gives you storage and mobility without masonry work.

Can these grills convert from propane to natural gas?

Several models on this list are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Island is explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is also natural gas convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner accepts both propane and natural gas as stated in its title. Always confirm that the conversion kit is included or available before purchase — running a propane-configured orifice on natural gas (or vice versa) is a safety hazard.

How much cooking space do these grills offer?

Cooking area varies significantly across this lineup. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner leads with 864 sq. in. of total cooking space and 48,000 BTU across its main burners, making it the right choice for large gatherings. Smaller built-in heads like the Hygrill STD 3-Burner and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are more compact by design and suit kitchens where counter real estate is limited. If BTU output matters, compare burner counts and total BTU ratings directly — the Brand-Man Built-In Head delivers 40,000 BTU from four burners in a 30-inch footprint.

Is a flat-top griddle a reasonable alternative to a traditional grate grill?

For smash burgers, breakfast foods, seafood, and anything that benefits from even surface contact, a flat-top delivers results a grate cannot. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle offers 34,000 BTU from two burners and is built from durable stainless steel with a lid — it functions as a permanent outdoor kitchen appliance rather than a novelty. The tradeoff is char and open-flame searing, which only grate-style grills provide. Many buyers install both in the same island to cover all cooking styles.

What grate material should you look for in an outdoor gas grill?

Porcelain-coated cast iron grates, like those on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and are relatively easy to clean, but the coating can chip over time if mishandled. Heavy-gauge stainless steel grates resist rust and hold up under sustained high heat without the chipping concern. 304 stainless steel — the grade specified on the Brand-Man Built-In Head — is the benchmark for outdoor cooking equipment because of its corrosion resistance in humid or coastal environments. Avoid thin stamped-steel grates regardless of coating; they warp and create uneven cooking surfaces.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output is the first number to cross-reference when shopping small outdoor gas grills, but raw heat alone is misleading without knowing how many burners are distributing it. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle delivers 34,000 BTUs across two burners — a high concentration per burner that translates to fast, even heat on a flat-top surface. By contrast, the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner spreads 40,000 BTUs across four burners, which gives you independent zone control but a lower per-burner intensity. For griddle-style cooking or searing, concentrated BTUs matter more; for low-and-slow cooking across multiple zones, burner count wins.

Material quality separates grills that last two seasons from those that last twenty years. Look for 304 stainless steel in the grill head, cooking grates, and burner tubes — the Brand-Man 4-Burner explicitly specifies 304 stainless, which resists salt-air corrosion far better than lower-grade 430 steel common in budget builds. Porcelain-coated grates, like those on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner, add a non-stick layer but require gentler cleaning to avoid chipping. For built-in or outdoor kitchen installations, a stainless lid is non-negotiable; the Stanbroil's lidded design protects the flat-top surface and helps retain heat during longer cooks.

Footprint and installation type define the category more than most buyers realize. True "small" gas grills in the built-in segment typically run 28–30 inches wide — the Stanbroil at 28 inches and the Brand-Man Head at 30 inches represent that range. If the grill will drop into an island or BBQ cabinet, confirm the cutout dimensions before ordering; a freestanding grill station like the Brand-Man Grill Island (which includes storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table) eliminates that concern entirely but takes up considerably more patio space. Natural gas convertibility is also worth confirming upfront — the Brand-Man 30-Inch Head and the Tytus Fresno both offer NG conversion, avoiding the ongoing cost of propane tank swaps.

Frequently asked questions

What is a realistic budget for a small outdoor gas grill in 2026?

Entry-level freestanding propane grills with four or more burners start around $519 — the Monument Grills 4+2-burner cabinet-style unit sits at that price point. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens typically start closer to $889, as seen with the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner head, and climb past $1,100 for premium stainless options like the Hygrill STD Series or Stanbroil 28-inch griddle. Full island stations with storage cabinets and side burners — such as the Brand-Man 4-burner grill island — land in the $800–$900 range, while larger semi-portable island grills like the Tytus Fresno push toward $1,400.

Can I convert a propane grill to natural gas for a permanent outdoor kitchen?

Several grills on this list are explicitly dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner grill head is listed as "Natural Gas Convertible," and the Brand-Man 4-burner grill island is described as "LPG/NG Convertible." The Tytus Fresno island grill is offered in both propane and natural gas configurations. Conversion typically requires a separate orifice kit and a licensed gas line connection — confirm kit availability with the manufacturer before purchasing if a permanent natural gas hookup is your goal. For more options built specifically around natural gas, see the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) guide.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series or the Stanbroil 28-inch griddle — is a drop-in cooking unit designed to be set into a pre-built BBQ island or outdoor kitchen frame. It has no legs or base cabinet of its own. A freestanding grill island, such as the Brand-Man 4-burner grill station or the Tytus Fresno, arrives as a complete unit with storage cabinets, counter space, and sometimes fold-out prep tables already integrated. Built-in heads offer more design flexibility; island stations are ready to use without additional construction. If you are building an outdoor kitchen from scratch, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) for a deeper comparison.

How many BTUs do I actually need for a small gas grill?

BTU output should be evaluated relative to cooking surface area, not as a standalone number. Among the grills listed here, the Stanbroil 28-inch griddle produces 34,000 BTUs across two burners, the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner head delivers 40,000 BTUs, and the Brand-Man grill island pushes 44,000 BTUs. The Tytus Fresno tops the group at 48,000 BTUs for its main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. For most residential cooking — burgers, steaks, vegetables — anything in the 34,000–44,000 BTU range is sufficient for a compact grill footprint. Higher BTU counts matter more when cooking at high volume or using a flat-top griddle surface that needs even heat saturation.

Which small gas grills have the highest user ratings?

Three grills in this lineup carry a perfect 5-out-of-5 rating: the Hygrill STD Series 3-burner built-in head (41 reviews), the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner grill head (72 reviews), and the Monument Grills 4+2-burner propane grill (71 reviews). The Brand-Man 4-burner grill island follows closely at 4.7 out of 5 across 113 reviews — the largest review pool in this group. The Stanbroil 28-inch griddle holds 4.5 out of 5 from 20 reviews, and the Tytus Fresno sits at 4.2 out of 5 from 16 reviews, reflecting its newer market presence.

Is a griddle-style gas grill a good choice for a small outdoor kitchen?

A flat-top griddle like the Stanbroil 28-inch 2-burner natural gas griddle suits a compact outdoor kitchen well when the cooking priority is breakfast foods, smash burgers, seafood, or stir-fry-style meals rather than traditional grill marks. At 34,000 BTUs across a stainless steel flat surface, it handles high-heat searing effectively. The trade-off is versatility — a griddle does not replicate open-grate grilling. Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a grill can reach temperature and recover heat after a cold cut of meat hits the grates. For most outdoor cooks, a main-burner total between 40,000 and 50,000 BTU is the practical sweet spot — enough power for high-heat searing without burning through fuel at an unreasonable rate. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU across its five main burners, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island sits at 44,000 BTU and the Brand-Man Built-In Head comes in at 40,000 BTU. A side burner matters if you want a dedicated zone for sauces or cast iron work; both the Tytus Fresno and the Brand-Man Island include one, rated at 12,000 BTU on the Tytus.

Cooking surface area determines how many proteins you can run simultaneously. The Tytus Fresno's 864 sq. in. is among the largest in this group — enough to handle full spatchcock birds alongside sides without rotating food every few minutes. Built-in and island-style grills with more modest footprints, like the 30-inch Brand-Man Built-In Head, trade raw square footage for a cleaner drop-in profile suited to a finished outdoor kitchen structure. For flat-top fans, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Natural Gas Griddle offers 34,000 BTU across two burners and a lid, covering an entirely different cooking style on the same plumbing line.

OUR TOP PICK

Shoppers comparing the full range of piped-in options will also find a broader selection on the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) page, which covers freestanding carts alongside built-in heads.

Frequently asked questions

What is a realistic price floor for a quality outdoor gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a freestanding cabinet-style grill with multiple burners. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner model at $519 sits at the entry point for serious outdoor grilling — anything cheaper typically sacrifices burner output, grate quality, or frame durability. Built-in grill heads and island units start higher: the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island starts at $799.99, while built-in heads like the Brand-Man 30-inch 304 stainless model run $889.99 and up.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and an island grill?

A built-in grill head, such as the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner or the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-Burner, is a drop-in unit designed to be installed into a masonry or prefabricated outdoor kitchen frame. An island grill, like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island, arrives as a complete freestanding station with its own cabinet base, storage, and sometimes a fold-out prep table. Island grills are faster to set up; built-in heads give you more design flexibility if you are building a custom outdoor kitchen.

Can these gas grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Most of the grills listed here are dual-fuel capable. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is explicitly sold as propane or natural gas compatible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island is labeled LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head is also natural gas convertible. The Hygrill STD Series is listed as natural gas. Always verify whether a conversion kit is included or sold separately before purchasing, and confirm your local gas supply before ordering. For more natural gas specific options, see the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) guide.

How much cooking surface do outdoor gas grills typically offer?

Cooking area varies significantly by price and format. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island offers 864 sq. in. of total cooking surface, which is generous enough for large gatherings. Smaller built-in heads and four-burner units generally land in the 500–700 sq. in. range. If flat-top cooking is the goal, the Stanbroil 28-inch 2-Burner Flat Top Griddle provides a dedicated griddle surface rather than grate-style cooking. For outdoor kitchen builds specifically, the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) page covers sizing considerations in more depth.

Which outdoor gas grill has the highest BTU output?

Among the products listed here, the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island leads with 48,000 BTU from the main burners plus an additional 12,000 BTU side burner, totaling 60,000 BTU. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island delivers 44,000 BTU, and the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head puts out 40,000 BTU. Higher BTU does not always mean better cooking — grate material, lid seal, and heat distribution matter equally — but raw output becomes important when you are cooking for large groups or searing at high temperatures.

Are stainless steel grill grates better than porcelain-coated grates?

Both materials are viable on high-quality grills, but they behave differently. Stainless steel grates, used on models like the Brand-Man 30-inch 304 stainless built-in head, are durable, easy to clean, and resistant to rust when properly maintained. Porcelain-coated grates, as found on the Tytus Fresno Island, retain heat well and resist sticking, but can chip over time if subjected to rough handling or thermal shock. For most buyers, build quality of the grate itself matters more than the coating material. If you are comparing grill options more broadly, the Best Barbeque Grills Natural Gas (2026) guide covers grate and material comparisons across a wider range of models.

VIEW ON AMAZON →

Material quality determines how a grill ages, not just how it looks on delivery day. The Brand-Man Built-In Head uses 304 stainless steel — the same alloy specified in commercial kitchen equipment — which resists pitting and surface rust better than 430-grade alternatives common at lower price points. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle also specifies premium stainless construction, and its flat-top design fills a different need than grate-style burners: consistent surface contact for smash burgers, vegetables, or fish that would otherwise fall through traditional grates.

For cooks planning a full outdoor kitchen setup, the decisions around grill selection overlap heavily with cabinet layout, countertop depth, and utility connections. Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen covers how to match grill head dimensions and BTU requirements to a built-in island. If you're still deciding between natural gas and propane — or want a broader view of what the NG category offers at different price points — Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas and the Natural Gas Outdoor Grills hub provide useful context before you finalize a purchase.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality outdoor gourmet gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a grill with meaningful cooking area, multiple burners, and stainless steel construction. The Monument Grills 4+2-burner propane cabinet grill at $519 represents the realistic entry point for a full-featured outdoor gourmet setup. Below that price range, BTU output, grate quality, and cabinet storage typically drop off sharply. Island-style units with built-in heads and storage cabinets — like the Brand-Man 4-burner station at $799.99 or the Tytus Fresno 5-burner island at $1,399.99 — carry higher price tags that reflect the added infrastructure.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head, such as the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner propane/natural gas head ($889.99) or the Hygrill STD Series 3-burner head ($1,129.99), is designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen counter or BBQ island — it has no legs or cart of its own. A freestanding island grill, like the Tytus Fresno 5-burner ($1,399.99) or the Brand-Man 4-burner gas island station ($799.99), arrives as a complete unit with its own cabinet base, storage, and sometimes a fold-out prep table. If you already have or are building a permanent outdoor kitchen structure, a built-in head gives a cleaner finish. If you want a portable or self-contained setup, the island-style unit is the simpler path.

Can these grills run on natural gas, or do they require propane?

Several models on this list are dual-fuel capable. The Tytus Fresno 5-burner is listed as propane or natural gas. The Brand-Man 4-burner gas island station is LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head is also natural gas convertible. The Stanbroil 28-inch built-in griddle and the Hygrill STD Series 3-burner head are listed specifically for natural gas. The Monument Grills 4+2-burner cabinet grill is a propane unit. Before purchasing, confirm which fuel type your outdoor space is plumbed for, since natural gas conversions typically require a specific orifice kit — and not all manufacturers include one in the box.

How does the Hygrill STD Series compare to the Brand-Man built-in head?

Both are built-in grill heads targeting outdoor kitchen installations. The Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner head ($889.99) outputs 40,000 BTU and is made from heavy-duty 304 stainless steel with natural gas convertibility. The Hygrill STD Series 3-burner ($1,129.99) is a natural gas unit rated as heavy duty with a stainless steel grill head and holds a perfect 5/5 rating across 41 reviews. The Hygrill costs $240 more but has fewer burners, suggesting its advantage lies in build quality and material thickness rather than raw output. For buyers prioritizing long-term durability in a permanent outdoor kitchen, the Hygrill is worth the premium. For those who want more simultaneous cooking zones at a lower price, the Brand-Man head makes a strong case.

Is a flat-top griddle a better choice than a traditional grill for outdoor kitchens?

It depends entirely on cooking style. The Stanbroil 28-inch 2-burner natural gas griddle ($1,109.99) delivers a 34,000 BTU flat cooking surface ideal for smash burgers, breakfast foods, stir-fry, and anything that benefits from full-surface contact and fat retention. Traditional grill grates — like those on the Monument Grills or the Brand-Man units — provide direct flame exposure, char marks, and better fat drainage. Many serious outdoor kitchens install both. If choosing just one, a griddle handles a broader range of foods but sacrifices classic grilled flavor; a grate-style grill is more versatile for steaks, chicken, and vegetables but can't cook eggs or pancakes without accessories.

Do any of these grills have side burners, and why does that matter?

Yes. The Tytus Fresno 5-burner island includes a 12,000 BTU side burner alongside its 48,000 BTU main cooking area. The Brand-Man 4-burner gas island station also includes a side burner. The Monument Grills 4+2-burner unit features infrared side sear burners in addition to standard side burners. A side burner matters for outdoor cooking completeness — it lets you run sauces, boil corn, or sauté without making a separate trip indoors, which is the whole point of a self-contained outdoor kitchen setup.

As a practical baseline, 600 sq. in. handles most gatherings of six to eight; anything smaller works for weeknight cooking but struggles at a party.

BTU output matters, but only in relation to cooking surface area. Raw BTU numbers without a surface-area denominator are marketing noise. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island delivers 44,000 BTU main output across its grate; the Tytus Fresno pushes 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner — a meaningful advantage when you need a sauce reduction or wok work happening at the same time as a full grill. Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a gas grill built for an outdoor kitchen?

Expect to spend at least $500–$800 for a complete grill island station with storage. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner cabinet-style unit comes in at $519 and is a functional starting point, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner hits $799.99 and adds more BTUs, fold-out prep space, and a higher review count (4.7/5 across 113 reviews). Drop-in or built-in grill heads — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head at $889.99 — require a separate enclosure, so factor in island construction costs on top of the grill price.

What's the difference between a drop-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A drop-in or built-in grill head is designed to be set into a custom or prefab outdoor kitchen frame — it has no cabinet of its own. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head and the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In Grill Head are both examples. A freestanding island grill like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island comes with its own cabinets and structure, so it's ready to use on a patio with no additional construction. Built-in heads generally give more design flexibility; island stations are quicker to deploy.

Can these grills convert between propane and natural gas?

Several models are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is advertised as natural gas convertible from propane. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is offered in propane or natural gas. If you're planning a permanent outdoor kitchen with a hard-plumbed gas line, confirm the specific model ships with a natural gas orifice kit or that one is available separately before purchasing.

How much cooking area do outdoor kitchen grills typically offer?

Cooking surface on these models ranges from roughly 600 sq. in. on compact built-in heads up to 864 sq. in. on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill — enough to handle 30-plus burgers at once. For a household that regularly entertains 10 or more people, aim for 700 sq. in. or more. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle takes a different approach: its flat-top surface suits large batches of pancakes, smash burgers, or stir-fry rather than traditional grate grilling.

Is stainless steel construction worth the premium in an outdoor kitchen grill?

For a permanent outdoor kitchen installation, yes — stainless steel holds up against rain, humidity, and grease far better than painted steel. The Hygrill STD Series Built-In Grill Head, the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head (304 stainless steel), and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle all use stainless construction. 304-grade stainless is the benchmark to look for; it contains nickel, which gives it superior corrosion resistance compared to lower-grade 430 stainless commonly found on budget units.

Which model is best if the outdoor kitchen already has a built-in island frame?

If the enclosure is already built, a drop-in head is the cleanest solution. The Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In Grill Head ($1,129.99, 5/5 from 41 reviews) and the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head ($889.99, 5/5 from 72 reviews) are both purpose-built for this scenario. The Brand-Man offers 40,000 BTUs across four burners; the Hygrill is a heavy-duty three-burner unit priced slightly higher, which often reflects thicker steel gauges and more robust construction. Match the cutout dimensions in your island frame to the grill head's specified drop-in size before ordering.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality outdoor gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a full-featured standalone grill with multiple burners and a stainless steel cabinet. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 sits at that floor and still delivers infrared side sear burners, a Clearview lid, and a built-in thermometer. Serious built-in grill heads and island stations start closer to $800–$900 and climb past $1,400 for premium configurations like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island at $1,399.99.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head or the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner — drops into a countertop cutout in a custom or modular outdoor kitchen. You supply the surround. A freestanding island grill such as the Tytus Fresno or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island arrives as a complete station with its own cabinet enclosure, storage, and side burner, so it can stand alone on a patio without any additional construction.

Can these gas grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Most models on this page are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is listed as natural gas convertible from propane. The Tytus Fresno ships ready for either propane or natural gas. The Hygrill STD Series and Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are sold as dedicated natural gas units, so verify the listing SKU before purchasing if you need propane compatibility.

How much cooking area do I actually need?

A household that grills for four to six people regularly is well served by 400–600 sq. in. of primary cooking surface. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island provides 864 sq. in. of total cooking area, making it appropriate for large gatherings or commercial-adjacent use. If space or budget is tighter, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner offer generous capacity without reaching that scale. For dedicated flat-top cooking, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle covers a 28-inch surface with two burners at 34,000 BTUs — suited for breakfast-style outdoor cooking alongside a primary grill.

Which grill has the highest BTU output on this page?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island leads with 48,000 BTU from its main burners plus a separate 12,000 BTU side burner, totaling 60,000 BTU across the station. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island follows at 44,000 BTU main output, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head delivers 40,000 BTU across four burners. Higher BTU matters most for searing and recovery speed after the lid opens — it is less critical for low-and-slow cooking.

Are these grills suitable for a permanent outdoor kitchen installation?

Built-in heads like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner and the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner are purpose-designed for permanent drop-in installation and use 304 stainless steel construction to handle continuous outdoor exposure. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle is similarly built for BBQ island integration. Island stations like the Tytus Fresno are described as semi-portable, meaning they can be repositioned but are heavy enough to function as permanent fixtures. If building a fixed outdoor kitchen, pairing a dedicated built-in head with a matching griddle gives the most flexibility — see the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen guide for a deeper comparison.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output is the starting point for evaluating any outdoor kitchen gas grill, but raw heat numbers need context. A 3-burner built-in like the Hygrill STD Series delivers its heat across a compact footprint, while a 5-burner island such as the Tytus Fresno spreads 48,000 BTU across 864 sq. in. of cooking surface plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner. The right figure depends on how much food you're moving at once — a backyard setup feeding four needs far less firepower than a full outdoor kitchen running appetizers, mains, and sides simultaneously. As a baseline, look for at least 10,000 BTU per burner; anything materially below that struggles with searing temperatures on cold or windy days.

Construction grade separates grills that survive three seasons from ones that corrode after one. The Hygrill STD Series and the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head both specify 304 stainless steel — a meaningful distinction because 304 resists salt air and grease far better than the 430-grade steel found in budget units. Grate material matters too: porcelain-coated grates (as on the Tytus Fresno) retain heat well and resist sticking, though they can chip if struck, while straight stainless grates are more durable but require more active maintenance to stay non-stick.

For island builds, storage and prep space are functional requirements, not bonuses. The Brand-Man 4-Burner island bundles storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table into a single 44,000 BTU station — everything needed for a self-contained outdoor cook setup without sourcing separate cabinetry. Convertibility between LPG and natural gas is equally practical: a grill that ships ready for both fuel types protects the investment if utility hookups change or if the setup moves. The Stanbroil 28-inch griddle takes a different approach entirely, offering a flat-top cooking surface with a lid for buyers who want smash burgers and breakfast sides alongside traditional grate cooking.

Frequently asked questions

What is a realistic price floor for a quality outdoor kitchen gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a cabinet-style freestanding unit with meaningful BTU output and stainless steel construction. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner model at $519 sits at that entry point. Built-in grill heads for drop-in island installations start closer to $890 (Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner head) and climb past $1,100 for heavier-duty options like the Hygrill STD Series or the Stanbroil 28-inch griddle head. Full island stations with cabinetry already integrated — such as the Tytus Fresno at $1,399.99 — carry the highest price tags but eliminate separate enclosure costs.

Can these grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Most of the grills listed here are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island, the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head, and the Tytus Fresno are all explicitly rated as LPG/NG convertible, meaning they ship with or support conversion hardware for natural gas line hookup. The Hygrill STD Series is listed specifically as a natural gas unit. Always confirm which fuel configuration ships standard before ordering, and check whether a conversion kit is included or sold separately.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a full grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner or the Hygrill STD Series — is a cooking unit only, designed to drop into a custom-built or prefabricated enclosure you already own or plan to build. A full grill island or station arrives with the cabinetry, storage, and side surfaces integrated. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island and the Tytus Fresno both include storage cabinets and prep surfaces out of the box. If you are starting from scratch without an existing enclosure, an all-in-one island station is typically the faster path to a finished outdoor kitchen.

How much cooking area do these grills offer?

Cooking surface varies meaningfully across this group. The Tytus Fresno provides 864 sq. in. of total cooking area across its 5-burner configuration, which is generous enough for large gatherings. Built-in heads like the Brand-Man 30-inch and Hygrill STD Series are sized for standard island cutouts and offer more moderate primary cooking surfaces. The Stanbroil 28-inch griddle provides a flat-top format rather than grate-style cooking, which changes how you use the space. Match the cooking area to your typical cook count — 500 to 600 sq. in. handles most family grilling, while 800+ sq. in. suits frequent entertaining.

Is the Hygrill STD Series worth the premium over the Brand-Man built-in head?

Both carry a 5/5 rating, but the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 is specifically engineered as a heavy-duty built-in with a 3-burner configuration, while the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner head comes in at $889.99 with one additional burner. The Hygrill's higher price likely reflects heavier-gauge 304 stainless construction and build quality aimed at permanent outdoor kitchen installations. If burner count and upfront cost are priorities, the Brand-Man head offers more burners for less money. If long-term durability in a built-in application is the deciding factor, the Hygrill STD's positioning as a purpose-built heavy-duty unit justifies the difference for many buyers.

Can I add a griddle surface to my outdoor kitchen instead of a traditional grill?

Yes — the Stanbroil 28-inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle is purpose-built for exactly that use case. At $1,109.99, it delivers 34,000 BTUs across a 2-burner flat-top surface with a lid, designed to drop into a BBQ island just like a conventional grill head. A flat-top griddle excels at breakfast items, smash burgers, fish, and vegetables that fall through standard grates. It is a meaningful functional trade-off: you gain versatility for certain food types but lose direct-flame char. Some outdoor kitchens pair a griddle station alongside a separate grill head to cover both cooking styles.

Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. Cooking, 48,000 BTU Main + 12,000 BTU Side Burner, Porcelain Grates, Storage Cabinets, Semi-Portable - GM/Ash Stone

Built for serious outdoor entertainers who want a full island setup without permanent installation, the Tytus Fresno delivers 864 square inches of cooking space across five burners putting out 48,000 BTU, plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner for sauces or sides. The porcelain-coated grates retain heat well and resist staining, and the GM/Ash Stone finish gives it a premium built-in aesthetic. Dual-fuel compatibility — propane or natural gas — means it adapts to whatever supply is already on your patio.

At $1,399.99, it's the priciest option on this list, and with only 16 reviews it has a narrower feedback base than comparable models. The semi-portable design is a selling point for some buyers but may feel like a compromise for those who want either a fully mobile cart or a true permanent island installation.

Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner - 44,000 BTU Stainless Steel BBQ Grill Station with Storage Cabinets, Fold-out Prep Table, LPG/NG Convertible, Black

The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island is the strongest all-around value on this list, combining 44,000 BTU output, a side burner, fold-out prep table, and storage cabinets into a self-contained station at $799.99. Its 4.7-star rating across 113 reviews — the largest review pool here — reflects consistently satisfied buyers. LPG/NG convertibility means no compromises on fuel source, and the stainless steel construction holds up to outdoor exposure without special treatment.

The black finish, while sharp-looking, can show grease buildup more visibly than brushed stainless alternatives. Buyers wanting a built-in appearance for a permanent outdoor kitchen will find the freestanding island format limiting, since it sits on a cart frame rather than integrating flush with masonry or cabinetry.

Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen

For buyers building a permanent outdoor kitchen who want a drop-in grill head rather than a freestanding unit, this Brand-Man 30-inch built-in is the obvious answer. The 304 stainless steel construction is a meaningful spec — 304 is a marine-grade alloy that resists corrosion from humidity and grease better than lower-grade stainless. At 40,000 BTU across four burners and with natural gas convertibility, it pairs well with an existing gas line. Its perfect 5-star rating across 72 reviews adds confidence.

At $889.99, the price is reasonable for a built-in head, but buyers must factor in the cost of surrounding cabinetry, countertops, and installation labor — none of which are included. It also lacks an integrated side burner, so anyone wanting that functionality needs to plan for a separate side burner unit in the island build.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner is aimed at propane users who want infrared searing capability without paying premium-tier prices. The Clearview® lid lets you monitor food without lifting the lid and losing heat, and dedicated infrared side sear burners deliver the high-temperature crust that standard burners struggle to match. A built-in thermometer and intuitive knob controls make it accessible for cooks at any skill level. At $519, it's the most affordable full-feature option here, and its 5-star rating from 71 reviewers is hard to argue with.

The propane-only configuration is the primary limitation — there's no natural gas convertibility listed, which rules it out for buyers with an existing NG line. The blue color finish is distinctive but polarizing; it won't suit every patio aesthetic, and color options appear limited.

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

The Hygrill STD Series targets buyers prioritizing build quality over burner count. A 5-star rating from 41 reviewers and a natural gas–native design (no conversion required) make it a straightforward choice for permanent outdoor kitchens already plumbed with NG. The "heavy duty" designation and stainless steel construction suggest a grill built to stay outdoors year-round without premature degradation. At $1,129.99, it sits in a professional-adjacent price tier that signals genuine material and hardware investment.

Three burners cover a smaller cooking zone than the four- and five-burner options on this list, which matters when cooking for larger groups. Buyers who regularly host parties of eight or more may find the capacity tight. The price is also steep relative to the Brand-Man built-in head, which delivers four burners and 40,000 BTU at $240 less.

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle fills a specific gap: buyers who want flat-top cooking in a permanent outdoor kitchen rather than a traditional grate. At 34,000 BTU across two burners and a full stainless steel construction, it handles breakfast foods, smash burgers, stir-fry, and seafood with a surface type that a conventional grill simply can't replicate. The included lid protects the griddle surface when not in use. Its 4.5-star rating from 20 reviewers is solid, especially for a specialized format.

This is not a replacement for a conventional grill — there's no grate cooking, no searing via open flame, and no side burner. Buyers expecting traditional BBQ results will be disappointed. The 20-review count is also the thinnest on this list outside the Tytus, so long-term durability data is still accumulating. For outdoor kitchens with space for multiple cooking stations, it works best alongside a separate grill head rather than as the sole cooking surface.

Buying guide and further reading

Total cooking area is the first number to check, but it needs context. The Tytus Fresno delivers 864 sq. in. of cooking surface — enough for a full brisket alongside a rack of ribs without rotation. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Brand-Man Built-In Head sit in the 4-burner range with somewhat smaller footprints, which suits tighter outdoor kitchen builds. If a griddle-style cook is the goal, the Stanbroil 28-Inch flat-top offers 34,000 BTU across two burners on a dedicated flat surface — a fundamentally different tool than a grate grill, so treat it as a specialty purchase rather than an all-rounder.

BTU output sets the ceiling for sear temperatures and recovery speed after the lid opens. The Tytus Fresno posts 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner — competitive figures for a five-burner island. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island comes in at 44,000 BTU total, and the Brand-Man Built-In Head at 40,000 BTU. The Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built-In and Monument Grills 4+2 Burner round out the field; the Monument's infrared side sear burners add targeted high-heat capability that raw BTU totals don't fully capture. As a rule, anything above 40,000 BTU across four or more burners handles backyard volume cooking without struggling on cold days.

OUR TOP PICK

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. Cooking, 48,000 BTU Main + 12,000 BTU Side Burner, Porcelain Grates, Storage Cabinets, Semi-Portable - GM/Ash Stone nails the largest cooking area in this lineup at 864 sq. in., the highest combined BTU output at 60,000 total, and genuine LP/NG dual-fuel flexibility with built-in storage cabinets for a self-contained outdoor station. $1399.99

VIEW ON AMAZON →

Fuel flexibility matters more than it used to. Every grill here except the Hygrill STD Series is either natively dual-fuel or convertible between LPG and natural gas. If a permanent natural gas line runs to the patio, a convertible or NG-native unit eliminates tank swaps and running-out-mid-cook problems. Built-in or drop-in head units like the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In and the Hygrill STD are designed for permanent outdoor kitchen integration, while freestanding island grills like the Tytus Fresno offer semi-portability — relevant if the outdoor kitchen layout is still evolving. Grate material rounds out the checklist: porcelain-coated cast iron (as on the Tytus) retains heat well and resists sticking, while 304 stainless steel grates on the Brand-Man Built-In resist corrosion in coastal or humid climates.

For buyers building a permanent outdoor cooking setup, the deep-dive comparisons on Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) and Best Barbeque Grills Natural Gas (2026) cover installation considerations, drop-in versus freestanding configurations, and a broader field of natural gas options worth cross-referencing before committing to a purchase.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality outdoor BBQ gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a capable freestanding unit. The Monument Grills 4+2 burner cabinet-style grill at $519 sits at that floor and delivers infrared side sear burners, a clearview lid, and a built-in thermometer at that price. Below $500, build quality and BTU output tend to drop noticeably. Island-style setups with storage cabinets and side burners start closer to $800–$900, as seen with the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 and the Brand-Man Built-In Head at $889.99.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head or the Hygrill STD Series — is a drop-in unit designed to be set into a custom or prefabricated outdoor kitchen counter. It has no legs or cabinet of its own. A freestanding island grill, such as the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island, arrives as a complete station with integrated storage cabinets and a prep surface, ready to use without additional construction.

Can these gas grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Several models on this list are dual-fuel capable. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island is explicitly LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man Built-In Head is listed as natural gas convertible. The Tytus Fresno is sold as propane or natural gas. The Hygrill STD Series and Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are dedicated natural gas units. Always confirm whether a conversion kit is included or sold separately before purchasing, and check local code requirements for permanent natural gas connections.

How much cooking area do these grills provide, and how do I size for my needs?

A general rule: plan for roughly 80–100 sq. in. of primary cooking area per guest at a typical cookout. The Tytus Fresno leads the list at 864 sq. in., which comfortably handles 8–10 people at once. Mid-range island grills typically land in the 600–700 sq. in. range. For serious outdoor kitchen setups serving larger gatherings, or for those who also want a flat-top surface, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle adds 34,000 BTUs of griddle cooking as a complement or alternative to a traditional grill.

Which grill on this list has the highest BTU output?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner puts out the most combined heat — 48,000 BTU across the main burners plus an additional 12,000 BTU side burner. For a built-in head, the Brand-Man 30-Inch registers 40,000 BTUs, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island delivers 44,000 BTUs total. Higher BTU numbers matter most for searing and cold-weather cooking, but heat retention from quality grates — such as the porcelain grates on the Tytus Fresno — plays an equally important role in real-world performance.

Are island-style outdoor grills difficult to move or reposition?

Most island grills are semi-permanent by design. The Tytus Fresno is specifically described as semi-portable, which means it can be relocated but is not intended for frequent moves the way a cart-style grill is. Units with storage cabinets and side burners are heavy and bulky; once positioned and connected to a natural gas line, relocation requires disconnecting the gas supply and potentially involves two people and a dolly. If flexibility of placement is a priority, a freestanding cart grill is a better fit than an island-style station.
